mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS below the prior 3-month average lowers discount rates and supports equity multiples, so S&P 500 20-day returns are positive when policy rates are falling. The signal is strongest when inflation is not accelerating, because rate cuts then read as genuine easing rather than panic.
failure_condition: If triggered observations do not show positive 20d S&P 500 returns more often than negative returns, or if the signal turns negative when CPIAUCSL is stable or falling, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals labor-market deterioration, weaker household income growth, and lower expected earnings, which should push S&P 500 returns negative over the next month. The effect should appear only after the unemployment rate has clearly turned up, not during flat labor conditions.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward SPX returns are not negative on average after the trigger, or if positive returns dominate despite rising UNRATE and stagnating PAYEMS.

mechanism_narrative: A broad acceleration in PAYEMS together with rising RSAFS indicates improving labor income and retail demand, which should lift near-term S&P 500 returns through better sales expectations and cyclical risk appetite. The effect is strongest when both series improve together rather than separately.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising PAYEMS and rising RSAFS does not outperform single-series signals, or if triggered joint-strength episodes fail to generate positive 20d S&P 500 returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly rise in INDPRO signals stronger industrial output and easing supply constraints, which should support equity returns by improving expected revenues and lowering recession odds. The signal should work over a one-week to one-month horizon when production is materially above its recent trend.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above its 3-month average by at least 0.4%
failure_condition: Reject if sharp INDPRO upswings are not followed by positive short-horizon SPX returns, or if the effect is absent once temporary revision noise is excluded.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in CPIAUCSL combined with a flat or rising FEDFUNDS implies tighter real financial conditions, lower valuation multiples, and weaker forward equity performance. The negative effect should be strongest when inflation surprises are not matched by offsetting policy easing.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises above its 6-month average while FEDFUNDS does not fall
failure_condition: Reject if high-inflation, non-easing episodes do not correspond to lower 20d SPX returns, or if the sign becomes positive when both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS rise together.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in HOUST signals improving housing demand and downstream activity, which should support cyclicals and lift S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The effect is a slower-moving demand signal and is expected to be more reliable over 60 trading days than over shorter windows.
failure_condition: Reject if 60d forward SPX returns are not higher during persistent HOUST uptrends, or if the relationship reverses when retail sales are also improving.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS falls while UNRATE rises, payroll losses and higher slack confirm a labor downturn that should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. This is a recession-warning signal and should fail when unemployment rises only because labor force participation shifts without payroll destruction.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines over 2 months and UNRATE rises over the same window
failure_condition: Reject if combined payroll declines and unemployment increases do not precede below-median 20d SPX returns, or if the signal is positive in repeated downturn episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A negative GDP growth impulse in GDPC1 relative to the previous quarter should forecast weaker risk assets over the following month because slower aggregate demand lowers earnings expectations and raises recession probability. The signal is regime-specific and should be strongest when GDP weakness coincides with weak industrial production.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines versus the prior quarter
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDP contractions do not precede lower 60d SPX returns on average, or if the signal disappears when INDPRO is also weakening.
